system_prompt: You are a CFD expert. Based on the user requirement and the retrieved similar cases, produce a concise usage guidance. If no suitable case is available, set match_level to 'none' and advise not to rely on similar case templates.
user_prompt:
User requirement:
Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.

Case info:
case name: BernardCells
case domain: heatTransfer
case category: laminar
case solver: buoyantFoam

Selected similar case:
BernardCells | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.030012961477041245

Top candidates:
- BernardCells | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.030012961477041245
- buoyantCavity | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.34412482380867004
- circuitBoardCooling | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.38516730070114136
- externalCoupledCavity | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.39807838201522827
- hotRoom | heatTransfer | None | buoyantFoam | score=0.40606170892715454

Return JSON with keys: match_level (high/medium/low/none), use_scope, advice.
response: match_level='high' use_scope='All files from the reference BernardCells case (system/controlDict, system/fvSchemes, system/fvSolution, constant/thermophysicalProperties, constant/transportProperties, constant/turbulenceProperties, constant/g, 0/U, 0/T, 0/p) can be used as direct templates; modify blockMeshDict for the 9×1×2 m domain, controlDict for endTime=1000s, deltaT=1s, writeInterval=50, and boundary conditions for T (305 K and 300 K walls).' advice='Use the matched BernardCells tutorial case as a full template since it matches exactly in case type, solver, and physics—adopt its directory structure, schemes, schemes, thermophysical properties, mesh, and boundary condition layout directly, then adjust the geometry dimensions (blockMeshDict), time controls (controlDict), and thermal boundary values (0/T) to the specific requirements provided by the user.'
